newQuiz.stem: A: "I knocked for ten minutes but nobody answered." B: "They ___ been out; their car wasn't in the driveway either."
newQuiz.explanation: 由『沒人應門』且『車也不在』兩項證據,對過去做有把握的肯定推測,用 must have been(一定是出去了)。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ must have p.p.=對過去的肯定推測;can't have 是『不可能』語意相反,證據都指向他們出門了,故用 must have。

newQuiz.stem: The report is due tomorrow morning, so you ___ stay up all night if you start now—you still have plenty of time.
newQuiz.explanation: 後句『還有很多時間』表示『沒必要』熬夜,用 don't have to(不必)。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ mustn't=禁止(不可),don't have to=不必(沒必要);此處是說沒必要熬夜而非禁止熬夜,別把『不必』誤填成『不可』。

newQuiz.stem: She speaks French so fluently that she ___ have lived in Paris for years.
newQuiz.explanation: 由『法語極流利』這項證據做肯定推測,用 must have lived(一定曾住過)。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 流利暗示確曾久居,用 must have;can't have(不可能住過)與證據矛盾,might not(也許沒)語氣太弱。

newQuiz.stem: When I was a child, I ___ swim very well, but after years without practice, I can hardly stay afloat now.
newQuiz.explanation: 描述過去的能力用 could(過去能夠),與『小時候』及對比『現在不太行』相符。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 過去能力用 could;can 是現在的能力,與 When I was a child 的過去時間及後句對比衝突。

newQuiz.stem: It's pouring outside and he left without a coat—he ___ be freezing by now.
newQuiz.explanation: 由『大雨且沒帶外套』推得『現在一定凍壞了』,用 must be(一定是)。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 有證據的肯定推測用 must;can't be(不可能冷)與情境矛盾,別把推測的方向弄反。

newQuiz.stem: You ___ have woken me up at 5 a.m.—the meeting wasn't until noon, so I lost two hours of sleep for nothing.
newQuiz.explanation: needn't have p.p.=『當初不必做卻做了』,符合『其實不用那麼早叫醒我』的語境。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ needn't have p.p.=不必做卻做了(已做);don't have to 是現在不必,must not 是禁止,皆與『過去多此一舉』語意不同。

newQuiz.stem: A: "Whose umbrella is this?" B: "It ___ be Tom's—he's the only one who brought one today."
newQuiz.explanation: 由『只有他帶傘』這項唯一線索,做有把握的肯定推測,用 must be(一定是)。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 證據唯一指向 Tom,用 must be;can't be(不可能是)與線索相反,might be(可能)語氣不夠肯定。

newQuiz.stem: We ___ rather take the early train so that we won't get stuck in the morning rush hour.
newQuiz.explanation: would rather + 原形=寧願(做某事),符合『寧願搭早班車以免塞車』。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 固定句型是 would rather V;had rather 較罕用,常考的搭配是 would rather,別與 had better 混淆。

newQuiz.stem: You ___ leave your bike unlocked here; bikes get stolen in this neighborhood all the time.
newQuiz.explanation: 後句『常被偷』暗示有後果,警告語氣用 had better not(最好別,否則有後果)。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ had better not=最好別(含警告);don't have to / needn't 是『不必』語意完全不同,此句是勸阻而非說沒必要。

newQuiz.stem: The ground is completely dry this morning, so it ___ have rained during the night after all.
newQuiz.explanation: 由『地面全乾』推得『昨晚不可能下過雨』,對過去的否定推測用 can't have rained。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ can't have p.p.=對過去的否定推測(不可能…過);must have(一定下過)與『地乾』的證據相反。

newQuiz.stem: Passengers ___ fasten their seat belts before the plane takes off; it's an airline rule.
newQuiz.explanation: 『航空規定』表示強制義務,用 must(必須)。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 表規定義務用 must;may 是『許可』,語氣為『可以』而非『必須』,與 rule 的強制性不符。

newQuiz.stem: After years of training, a guide dog ___ guide a blind person safely across a busy street—an ability ordinary dogs will never have.
newQuiz.explanation: 表『能夠(具備能力)』用 be able to,主詞為單數第三人稱故 is able to;符合『導盲犬能安全帶領主人』。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 兩個情態/能力詞不可連用:must can、can be able to 皆重複;must able to 缺 be 動詞;正確的能力表達是 is able to。

newQuiz.stem: A: "He failed again even though he studied so hard." B: "He ___ have studied the wrong material; the test covered Chapter 5, not Chapter 4."
newQuiz.explanation: 由『考的是第5章而他像準備了別的』推得『一定是讀錯範圍』,對過去做肯定推測用 must have studied。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 對過去原因的肯定推測用 must have;can't have(不可能讀錯)與『他考砸了』的結果矛盾。

newQuiz.stem: When you finish the exam, you ___ leave the room quietly, but you ___ talk to other students.
newQuiz.explanation: 前空『可以安靜離場』表許可用 may;後空『不可與他人交談』表禁止用 mustn't。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ may=許可(可以),mustn't=禁止(不可);兩空一許可一禁止,別把 don't have to(不必)當成禁止填入後空。

newQuiz.stem: I'm not sure where Lily is. She ___ be in the library, or she ___ have gone home—I really can't tell.
newQuiz.explanation: 『我真的說不準』表示兩種情況都只是可能,前後皆用表不確定的 might(可能)。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 不確定的猜測用 might;must(一定)語氣太強,與『說不準』的猶豫語氣不符,兩空都該是 might。

newQuiz.stem: You look exhausted. You ___ have been driving for over ten hours straight—why didn't you take a break?
newQuiz.explanation: 由『你看起來累壞了』推得『一定是連開十幾小時』,對過去持續行為的肯定推測用 must have been driving。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ must have been V-ing=對過去進行動作的肯定推測;can't have(不可能開過)與『看起來很累』的證據相反。

newQuiz.stem: Visitors ___ touch the paintings, but they ___ take photos without flash if they like.
newQuiz.explanation: 前空『不可觸摸畫作』表禁止用 must not;後空『可以(不用閃光燈)拍照』表許可用 may。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ must not=禁止,may=許可;前禁止後許可,別把 don't have to(不必)誤用於『不可觸摸』。

newQuiz.stem: There's no way the thief came in through the window—it was locked from the inside. He ___ used the front door.
newQuiz.explanation: 既然窗戶從內反鎖、小偷不可能由窗進入,由排除法推得『一定是走前門』,用 must have used。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 排除其他可能後的肯定推測用 must have;can't have used(不可能用過前門)與『只剩前門可走』的推論矛盾。

newQuiz.stem: We've still got half a tank of gas, so we ___ stop to refuel until we reach the next city.
newQuiz.explanation: 由『還有半箱油』推得『到下個城市前不必停下加油』,表將來沒必要用 won't have to(不必)。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ won't have to=將來不必;mustn't(禁止加油)語意荒謬,此句是說沒必要停而非禁止停。

newQuiz.stem: A: "Sorry I'm late—the bus broke down." B: "You ___ taken a taxi; I told you this meeting was important."
newQuiz.explanation: should have p.p.=當初應該做卻沒做(責備/遺憾),符合『你當初應該搭計程車』的語境。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ should have p.p.=當初該做卻沒做;must have(一定是)是推測,needn't have(不必卻做了)語意相反,此句在責怪對方沒搭計程車。

newQuiz.stem: These instructions ___ be followed exactly; otherwise the machine ___ be damaged beyond repair.
newQuiz.explanation: 前空『必須確實遵守』為強制義務用 must(被動 must be followed);後空『機器可能損壞』表可能性用 may(may be damaged)。
newQuiz.trap: ⚠ 此題考情態動詞被動 must be done / may be done;前空義務用 must,後空表可能後果用 may,別把可能性的 may 與義務的 must 對調。
